records.ety: [F. relique, from L. reliquiae, pl., akin to relinquere to leave behind. See Relinquish.]

defs: That which remains; that which is left after loss or decay; a remaining portion; a remnant.
quotes: The fragments, scraps, the bits and greasy relics.

defs: The body from which the soul has departed; a corpse; especially, the body, or some part of the body, of a deceased saint or martyr; -- usually in the plural when referring to the whole body.
quotes: There are very few treasuries of relics in Italy that have not a tooth or a bone of this saint.
Thy relics, Rowe, to this fair urn we trust, And sacred place by Dryden's awful dust.

defs: Hence, a memorial; anything preserved in remembrance; as, relics of youthful days or friendships.
quotes: The pearls were spilt; Some lost, some stolen, some as relics kept.
